title: Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Duplicate Minima, Invalid Edge Handling

tags: Binary Search on Rotated Array with Duplicate Elements
Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Duplicate Minima

statement.markdown: Fix the bug in the provided class method. Given a rotated sorted array with duplicates, return the minimum value. If the array is empty or all elements are the same, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: A list of integers: The input array.
statement.outputFormat: An integer: The minimum value in the array or -1 if the array is empty or all elements are the same.
statement.notes: Ensure the time complexity of your solution is O(log n).

examples.explanation: The minimum value in the array is 1.
All elements in the array are the same.

hints.text: To find the minimum value, first determine the correct range for binary search. Then, make sure the edge cases of an empty array or an array with all the same elements are handled correctly.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: To find the minimum in a rotated sorted array with duplicates, we need to first determine the correct range for binary search. Then, handle edge cases such as an empty array or an array with all the same elements.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Sort the array and find the minimum.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the concept of duplicate minima to reduce the search space.
editorial.optimalSolution: Perform binary search to find the correct range and then compare elements to handle duplicate minima.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This is a proof of correctness for the algorithm.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run the algorithm, consider an example input.
commonMistakes: Forgot to handle edge cases of an empty array or an array with all the same elements.
Incorrectly implemented the binary search logic to find the correct range.
